title: Next Generation of Eva Fans
wikitext:
[]: Next Generation of Eva Fans             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 17, 2007 12:00 am i know this has probably already been talked about (and if there is a thread already talking about this, please send me a link), but.... how is the newer generation of Anime Fans going to react to the new Evangelion? especially the ones who havnt seen the original. i know i would be considered part of the newer generation (being that im only 14) but Evangelion is still my favorite out of all the other animes out there. (next being FullMetal Alchemist and Cowboy Bebop, another old 1)

[]:             Quote Post by Zeon » Mon Sep 17, 2007 12:17 am Honestly, 12 years isn't even that old... I do think it's sad that a lot of newer anime fans haven't watched true genre landmarks like Eva and are only watching the new computer-animated moe shit that comes out these days.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 17, 2007 12:20 am the 3D animation just gives it a more real look, they have to keep improving or else we'd still be drawing stick figures on cave walls

[]:             Quote Post by Zeon » Mon Sep 17, 2007 12:21 am I'd rather take cel animation any day. It's more genuine.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 17, 2007 12:23 am i only enjoy when they put 3D on the cartoons and not when they try to make it look completely real like in FF-XII

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Sep 17, 2007 1:16 am I hate the new 3D CGI animation (or whatever it's called). I've been watching the old Tenchi Muyo and the new one back to back, and the new one seems to have lost a lot of its "soul" during the transition to the new style.

[]: Re: Next Generation of Eva Fans             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Sep 17, 2007 2:35 am Sachiel_13 wrote: i know this has probably already been talked about (and if there is a thread already talking about this, please send me a link), but.... how is the newer generation of Anime Fans going to react to the new Evangelion? especially the ones who havnt seen the original.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 17, 2007 6:11 am It's not necessary to use cells at all, thanks to computers. You can get the cel look and effects with computers easily enough. I agree that computer effects can have a horribly cheesy effect in a number of places, especially in the new Star Wars movies. The plastic Star Destroyers and TIE fighters had an ambience that the very obviously CG models just ruin. I guess it's how glaringly obvious the computer effects are...if the CG nature of the scene isn't jarringly emphasized, it can turn out looking very good, actually.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Mon Sep 17, 2007 8:45 am Zeon wrote: Honestly, 12 years isn't even that old... I do think it's sad that a lot of newer anime fans haven't watched true genre landmarks like Eva and are only watching the new computer-animated moe shit that comes out these days.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Sep 17, 2007 9:27 am There's nothing wrong with CG. It's a tool in film and animation like everything else from editing to colors. The point is how you USE it. I haven't seen R'Eva so I can't comment on the CG in it but if its handled properly and isn't overly distracting than its done its job well.

[]:             Quote Post by Shawn Dudley » Mon Sep 17, 2007 3:36 pm Zeon wrote: Honestly, 12 years isn't even that old... I do think it's sad that a lot of newer anime fans haven't watched true genre landmarks like Eva and are only watching the new computer-animated moe shit that comes out these days. fo realz no more quality storyline, mixed with redheirings and conspiracies, I think it should be a anime heigharchy law that to be considered a true condiseur of anime evangelion is a must

[]:             Quote Post by Zeon » Mon Sep 17, 2007 3:42 pm Shawn Dudley wrote: Others get hooked on the story and fall in love with one or more of the characters. The fact that Yaoi has become popular with US fangirls as of late is also a surprisingly big draw to EVA, as the Kaworu cult is alive and well. My daughter's a good case in point: she went from Pokemon, to Inu-Yasha, to FMA, to EVA, and her interest in EVA is largely that of the 17th Angel. (sigh). It's not her most favorite currently (that would be Darker Than Black), but I guarantee that will change with the new EVA manga coming out.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Mon Sep 17, 2007 3:48 pm I wonder what the definition of the new wave of Eva fans is: Is it people that have just recently watched the series, or the people that will become fans after watching RoE? In any case, I do think it's sad that many people pass up on Eva simply because it's over ten years old. On MyAnimeList.net (a kind of MySpace for anime fans) the Evangelion fan club was only at about 25 when I joined (it's since changed, up to about 100 members now) but I was disapointed that clubs dedicated to Lolis and Claymore and Haruhi were much bigger. Also, about 500 people have Haruhi listed as their favorite show, while the figure is only about 200 for Eva. I feel that this is some kind of injustice and I hope RoE changes this BrikHaus wrote: I hate the new 3D CGI animation (or whatever it's called). I've been watching the old Tenchi Muyo and the new one back to back, and the new one seems to have lost a lot of its "soul" during the transition to the new style.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Sep 17, 2007 4:15 pm CitizenGeek wrote: On MyAnimeList.net (a kind of MySpace for anime fans)

[]:             Quote Post by JP » Mon Sep 17, 2007 7:06 pm I think CG is great. Look at Futurama, for example. It still has a very cartoony-simpsons feel, but the 3d helps achieve nicer visuals.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Sep 17, 2007 7:15 pm JP wrote: I think CG is great. Look at Futurama, for example. It still has a very cartoony-simpsons feel, but the 3d helps achieve nicer visuals.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Mon Sep 17, 2007 8:09 pm Haruhi will probably be the new Evangelion of this decade. I really, really liked Haruhi, but obviously, not as much so... Actually, in many anime circles these days, Eva tends to be something everyone has seen, but most dislike. Eva hasn't been getting much love in western circles as of late; we've moved on to bigger and better things (BAH). Anyway, if Rebuild starts a new legion of fans, which I highly doubt it will, because everyone saw Eva already, I certainly won't be the one teaching the newfags.

[]:             Quote Post by Shawn Dudley » Mon Sep 17, 2007 8:10 pm Whoa... I'm glad she's seen (and loves) Eva, but it's for all the wrong reasons! That's actually kind of disheartening.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 17, 2007 8:32 pm [quote="Shawn Dudley"] Honestly, I think a lot of guys watch EVA for the Bishojo, so it doesn't surprise me if girls watch it for Bishonen.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 17, 2007 8:38 pm Shawn Dudley wrote: Honestly, I think a lot of guys watch EVA for the Bishojo, so it doesn't surprise me if girls watch it for Bishonen.
